mission.title: flux: flux fails to bootstrap a repo in a different Windows drive than the one in $env:TEMP
mission.description: flux fails to bootstrap a repo in a different Windows drive than the one in $env:TEMP. This issue affects 13+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: flux fails to bootstrap a repo in a different Windows drive than the one in $env:TEMP

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Describe the bug

When trying to bootstrap a new flux repository on Windows, if the target directory is in a different drive than $env:TEMP, flux will fail to bootstrap it, with the error below:

steps.title: Apply the fix for flux fails to bootstrap a repo in a different Windows drive
steps.description:
When running `flux bootstrap` (or `flux install`) from a drive different than where `%TEMP%` lives (e.g. working dir on `D:\`, temp on `C:\`), Go's `filepath.Rel` returns an error because it can't compute a relative path across Windows drive letters. The CLI just propagated that error and died.

The
```yaml
github.com/pellegrino/test-flux2-windows on Ōśü’ĖÅ  (eu-central-1)
ŌØ» pwd

Path
----
D:\src\github.com\pellegrino\test-flux2-windows

github.com/pellegrino/test-flux2-windows on Ōśü’ĖÅ  (eu-central-1)
ŌØ» flux bootstrap github --owner=pellegrino --repository=my-repo --branch=main --path .\clusters\dok8s --personal
Ō¢║ connecting to github.com
Ō£ö repository cloned
Ō£Ü generating manifests
Ō£Ś generating install manifests failed: Rel: can't make C:\Users\VITORP~1\AppData\Local\Temp\flux-system445357215 relative to D:\src\github.com\pellegrino\test-flux2-windows
```

steps.title: Upgrade flux to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade flux:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade flux jetstack/flux --namespace cert-manager
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Confirm flux fails to bootstrap a repo in a different Wind is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: When running `flux bootstrap` (or `flux install`) from a drive different than where `%TEMP%` lives (e.g. working dir on `D:\`, temp on `C:\`), Go's `filepath.Rel` returns an error because it can't compute a relative path across Windows drive letters. The CLI just propagated that error and died.

The relative path conversion was originally added as a workaround for [kubernetes-sigs/kustomize#2789](. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
